interfaces.name: SlotFX
interfaces.signalFlowRole: hot-swappable network slot (selects which compiled network to load)

interfaces.name: TableProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: complex data exposed from compiled network

interfaces.name: SliderPackProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: complex data exposed from compiled network

interfaces.name: AudioSampleProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: complex data exposed from compiled network

parameterGroups.label: Voice Mode
parameterGroups.rationale: Inherited envelope base class parameters controlling polyphonic behaviour

parameters.name: Monophonic
parameters.description: Enables monophonic mode where only one voice is rendered

parameters.name: Retrigger
parameters.description: Restarts the envelope when a new note is triggered in monophonic mode

gaps.question: How does the envelope modulator load a compiled network and use it for per-voice envelope generation?
gaps.context: The network must produce a per-voice modulation signal.

gaps.question: How do startVoice/stopVoice/isPlaying interact with the compiled network? How does the network signal that a voice should be killed?
gaps.context: Envelope modulators control voice lifecycle - this is critical behaviour.

gaps.question: How are compiled network parameters exposed alongside the fixed Monophonic/Retrigger parameters?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the parameter offset.

gaps.question: Does the envelope run at control rate (downsampled) or audio rate?
gaps.context: Modulators typically run at control rate for efficiency.

gaps.question: How are Tables, SliderPacks, and AudioFiles from the compiled network exposed?
gaps.context: Complex data access for custom envelope shapes.

notes: Inherits Monophonic and Retrigger from EnvelopeModulator base class. All other parameters come from the compiled network. The network runs at control rate with single-channel output.
